name: ETH Value Recovery Scale-In v1
thesis: ETH 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing scale-in around value recovery when confirmation improves across bars instead of one-shot all-in timing. In a fully choppy regime, the edge should come from broad reclaim-of-value behavior rather than rare continuation events.
invalidation_condition: Abort if value-recovery participation is still too sparse, if recoveries stall immediately after reclaim, or if the confirmation add-on arrives too late to matter after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes through short-term value, reclaims it, and then stabilizes enough to justify adding on improved confirmation instead of taking maximum size immediately.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Fully choppy 4h conditions should create repeated flush-and-repair behavior. A small initial reclaim entry plus confirmation-based add-on tests management variation without depending on a single perfect bar.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is appropriate because reclaim structure is usually clean enough to define value recovery while still offering enough movement for scale-in management to matter.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the reclaim only bounces back to value and never extends, the add-on logic will either not trigger or will add into dead money.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the first reclaim works but the add-on hurts results, keep the reclaim entry and remove the scale-in expression before changing the setup.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; flush, reclaim, and confirmation improvement are sequential but broad
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed with moderate reclaim thresholds instead of rare capitulation rules

trade_management.add_on_condition: Next bar closes in trade direction and RSI_14 improves by at least 2 points while DI handoff remains intact

long: Stop loss: 0.9 * ATR_14 below average entry
Take profit: 0.65 * ATR_14 on 40% total size
Take profit: 1.0 * ATR_14 on 35% total size
short: Stop loss: 0.9 * ATR_14 above average entry
Take profit: 0.65 * ATR_14 on 40% total size
Take profit: 1.0 * ATR_14 on 35% total size
